items.passage: An ecosystem is a community of organisms interacting with their physical environment. Plants form the foundation of the system by capturing sunlight to produce food. Herbivores eat these plants, and predators in turn eat the herbivores. When organisms die, fungi and bacteria break down their remains and return important nutrients to the soil. This continuous cycle keeps energy flowing and supports a wide variety of life.

Climate change refers to long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns across the planet. Many of these recent shifts have been driven by gases released when people burn coal, oil, and natural gas for energy. These gases trap heat in the atmosphere, which slowly raises global temperatures over time. Warmer conditions affect rainfall, ice caps, and the seasons that animals depend on. Scientists study these changes to predict how communities should adapt.

The placebo effect occurs when patients feel better after taking a treatment that contains no active medicine. Researchers have observed this surprising response in studies of pain relief, sleep, and even some chronic conditions. Belief and expectation seem to trigger real changes in the brain that ease symptoms for many people. Doctors must therefore design careful trials in which neither patients nor staff know who received the real medicine. Understanding the placebo helps reveal how powerfully the mind shapes physical health.

Medieval guilds were associations of craft workers that organized trade in European towns for many centuries. Each guild set the standards for quality, training, and prices in a particular craft such as baking or weaving. Young apprentices joined a master for several years before they could open their own workshops. Guilds also offered support to members in times of sickness or hardship and helped fund local festivals. Although they often limited outside competition, guilds preserved valuable skills from one generation to the next.

The ocean floor contains many surprising features that remain hidden beneath miles of dark water. Long mountain ranges run through the middle of every ocean, marking the places where the planet's crust is slowly pulling apart. Deep trenches form near the edges of some continents, where one section of crust slides beneath another. Vast plains of soft sediment stretch between these features and shelter a quiet world of strange creatures. Modern sonar and submarines have allowed scientists to map this hidden landscape in remarkable detail.

A planetary atmosphere is the layer of gases that a planet holds in place through its gravity. Earth's atmosphere contains the oxygen that supports life and a thin layer that shields the surface from harmful rays. Mars has a much thinner atmosphere made mostly of carbon dioxide, leaving it cold and dry today. The giant planet Jupiter has a thick atmosphere with powerful storms that can last for centuries. Studying these different worlds helps scientists understand how atmospheres shape the conditions of each planet.
